---
title: "Unidad 10: Nginx - Servidor Web y Reverse Proxy"
subtitle: "Nginx de producción: configuración, reverse proxy, SSL y hardening"
author: "Diego Saavedra"
date: "2026-03-31"
date-format: medium
---

# Unidad 10: Nginx en Producción

## Introducción

Nginx es el servidor web más usado en internet y el estándar para reverse proxy. Su modelo asíncrono basado en eventos lo hace extremadamente eficiente para alto tráfico. En esta unidad vas a dominar Nginx desde la instalación hasta el hardening de producción.

## Arquitectura de Nginx

### Modelo Asíncrono vs Apache

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│               NGINX vs APACHE: ARQUITECTURA                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   APACHE (Modelo híbrido)         NGINX (Modelo asíncrono)    │
│  ┌─────────┐  ┌─────────┐         ┌─────────┐                  │
│  │Worker 1 │  │Worker 2 │         │         │                  │
│  │Thread 1 │  │Thread 2 │         │  Event  │ ← 1 proceso      │
│  │Request  │  │Request  │         │  Loop   │   maneja miles   │
│  │   A     │  │   B     │         │         │   de conexiones  │
│  └─────────┘  └─────────┘         └─────────┘                  │
│                                                                  │
│   1 request × thread              1 proceso = N conexiones     │
│   + overhead de threads           + bajo consumo memoria        │
│                                                                  │
│   Mejor para: legacy PHP          Mejor para: alto tráfico,     │
│                                    static files, reverse proxy  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Flujo de Request en Nginx

```
1. TCP Handshake (puerto 80/443)
       │
       ▼
2. Nginx Event Loop acepta conexión
       │
       ▼
3. Parse de headers HTTP
       │
       ▼
4. Matching de server_name (virtual host)
       │
       ▼
5. Matching de location (rewrite/filter/alias)
       │
       ▼
6. Handler: static file / proxy_pass / fastcgi
       │
       ▼
7. Escribir a access.log
       │
 ▼
Cierre o keep-alive
```

## Instalación en Producción

### Paquetes Recomendados

```bash
# Actualizar índice
$ sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
# <1>

# Instalar Nginx + utilidades
$ sudo apt install -y nginx nginx-extras \
    certbot python3-certbot-nginx \
    openssl logrotate
# <2>
```

1. **apt update**: Lista de paquetes actualizada
2. **nginx-extras**: Módulos adicionales (more headers, etc.)

### Verificar Instalación

```bash
# Estado del servicio
$ sudo systemctl status nginx --no-pager
● nginx.service - A high performance web server and a reverse proxy server
     Loaded: loaded (/lib/systemd/system/nginx.service; enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Tue 2026-03-31 10:00:00 UTC
# <1>

# Puertos en escucha
$ sudo ss -lntp | grep nginx
LISTEN 0 511 0.0.0.0:80  0.0.0.0:* users:("nginx",pid=1234,fd=6)
LISTEN 0 511 0.0.0.0:443 0.0.0.0:* users:("nginx",pid=1234,fd=8)
# <2>
```

1. **systemctl status**: Confirmar servicio activo
2. **Puertos 80 y 443**: Escuchando en todas las interfaces

### Prueba de Respuesta

```bash
# Headers básicos
$ curl -I http://localhost
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Server: nginx/1.24.0 (Ubuntu)
Content-Type: text/html
# <1>

# HTML completo
$ curl -s http://localhost | head -20
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<head>
<title>Welcome to nginx!</title>
...
# <2>
```

1. **curl -I**: Muestra headers de respuesta
2. **curl -s**: Descarga HTML completo

## Estructura de Archivos

```
/etc/nginx/
├── nginx.conf              # Config principal (includes others)
├── conf.d/                 # Configuraciones adicionales (*.conf)
├── sites-available/        # Server blocks disponibles
├── sites-enabled/         # Server blocks activos (symlinks)
├── snippets/               # Fragmentos reutilizables
├── modules-available/      # Módulos dinámicos
├── modules-enabled/        # Módulos activos
└── mime.types              # Tipos MIME

/var/log/nginx/
├── access.log              # Todos los requests
├── error.log               # Errores
├── site1.access.log       # Logs por sitio (si configurado)
└── site1.error.log
```

###nginx.conf: Estructura Principal

```nginx
user www-data;
worker_processes auto;          # Auto-detect CPU cores
pid /run/nginx.pid;
include /etc/nginx/modules-enabled/*.conf;

events {
    worker_connections 1024;    # Conexiones por worker
    multi_accept on;
    use epoll;                  # Linux optimized
}

http {
    # Basic settings
    sendfile on;
    tcp_nopush on;
    tcp_nodelay on;
    keepalive_timeout 65;
    types_hash_max_size 2048;

    # Logging
    access_log /var/log/nginx/access.log;
    error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log;

    # MIME types
    include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
    default_type application/octet-stream;

    # Include site configs
    include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;
    include /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/*;
}
```

## Server Blocks (VirtualHosts)

###Server Block Básico

```bash
# Crear estructura de directorios
$ sudo mkdir -p /var/www/mysite/{public,logs}

# Crear contenido
$ echo '<!DOCTYPE html><h1>My Site</h1>' | sudo tee /var/www/mysite/public/index.html

# Permisos
$ sudo chown -R www-data:www-data /var/www/mysite
# <1>
```

1. **Estructura**: Document root y logs separados

### Configuración de Server Block

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/mysite.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
    server_name mysite.local www.mysite.local;

    # Document root
    root /var/www/mysite/public;
    index index.html index.htm;

    # Access log específico
    access_log /var/www/mysite/logs/access.log;
    error_log /var/www/mysite/logs/error.log;

    # Directorio raíz
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    }

    # Directorios protegidos
    location /private/ {
        deny all;
        return 403;
    }

    # Static files con cache
    location ~* \.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|ico|css|js)$ {
        expires 30d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, no-transform";
    }

    # Prevenir acceso a archivos ocultos
    location ~ /\. {
        deny all;
    }
}
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Server block completo**: root, logs, seguridad, cache

### Habilitar y Verificar

```bash
# Habilitar (crear symlink)
$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/mysite.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/mysite.conf
# <1>

# Deshabilitar default (opcional)
$ sudo rm -f /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/default

# Validar configuración
$ sudo nginx -t
nginx: the configuration file /etc/nginx/nginx.conf syntax is ok
nginx: configuration file /etc/nginx/nginx.conf test is successful
# <2>

# Recargar
$ sudo systemctl reload nginx
```

1. **ln -sf**: Crea symlink para habilitar
2. **nginx -t**: Test de sintaxis

### Probar Server Block

```bash
# Con Host header
$ curl -H 'Host: mysite.local' http://127.0.0.1
<!DOCTYPE html><h1>My Site</h1>
# <1>

# Ver logs
$ sudo tail /var/www/mysite/logs/access.log
127.0.0.1 - - [31/Mar/2026:10:15:00 +0000] "GET / HTTP/1.1" 200 123 "-" "curl/8.1.0"
```

1. **curl -H**: Simula request con Host header

## Reverse Proxy

### Concepto

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    REVERSE PROXY NGINX                          │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                  │
│   INTERNET                  NGINX              BACKEND          │
│  ┌─────────┐            ┌─────────┐        ┌─────────┐          │
│  │ cliente │ ──────────> │ :80/:443│ ──────> │ :3000   │          │
│  │         │  request    │ proxy   │         │ (app)   │          │
│  └─────────┘            └─────────┘        └─────────┘          │
│                                                                  │
│   NGINX: load balancer, SSL terminator, cache, logs centralizados│
│   BACKEND: Node, Python, Go, Java - solo localhost              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Reverse Proxy a Aplicación

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/api-proxy.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;

    # Proxy a backend
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        
        # Headers esenciales
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        
        # Timeouts
        proxy_connect_timeout 60s;
        proxy_send_timeout 60s;
        proxy_read_timeout 60s;
    }

    # Health check endpoint
    location /health {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000/health;
        access_log off;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/api-proxy.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
# <1>
```

1. **Proxy completo**: Headers, timeouts, health check

### Proxy a Múltiples Backends (Load Balancing)

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/loadbalancer.conf <<'EOF'
upstream backend {
    least_conn;  # Menos conexiones
    
    server 10.0.1.11:3000 weight=5;
    server 10.0.1.12:3000 weight=3;
    server 10.0.1.13:3000 weight=2;
}

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://backend;
        
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/loadbalancer.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx
# <1>
```

1. **Load balancing**: round-robin, least_conn, IP hash

### WebSocket Support

```bash
location /ws/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    
    # WebSocket headers
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
    
    # Timeouts extendidos
    proxy_read_timeout 3600s;
    proxy_send_timeout 3600s;
}
```

## SSL/TLS con Certbot

### Instalación de Certbot

```bash
# Instalar certbot y plugin nginx
$ sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y certbot python3-certbot-nginx
# <1>
```

1. **certbot**: Automated TLS con Let's Encrypt

### Obtener Certificado

```bash
# Obtener certificado (dominio debe apuntar al servidor)
$ sudo certbot --nginx -d example.com -d www.example.com
# <1>

# Following prompts:
# - Enter email (for expiry notices)
# - Agree ToS (A)
# - Share email (optional)
# - Redirect HTTP to HTTPS (select 1 or 2)
# - Done!
```

1. **--nginx**: Auto-configura Nginx con SSL

### Configuración SSL Generada

```bash
# Ver certificado
$ sudo ls -la /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/
fullchain.pem  privkey.pem  cert.pem  chain.pem

# Ver configuración nginx
$ cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/default-ssl.conf
# <1>
```

1. **Archivos SSL**: Certificado, clave, chain

### SSL de Producción Manual

```bash
# Crear configuración SSL manual (si certbot no funciona)
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf <<'EOF'
# SSL Certificate
ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;

# SSL Settings - Protocolos seguros
ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off;

# SSL Ciphers
ssl_ciphers ECDHE-ECDSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256:ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384;

# HSTS
add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000; includeSubDomains; preload";

# OCSP Stapling
ssl_stapling on;
ssl_stapling_verify on;
resolver 8.8.8.8 8.8.4.4 valid=300s;
resolver_timeout 5s;
EOF
```

### Renovación Automática

```bash
# Test de renovación
$ sudo certbot renew --dry-run
# <1>

# Ver cronjob
$ sudo cat /etc/cron.d/certbot
# /etc/cron.d/certbot: certbot renewal cronjob
0 */12 * * * root test -x /usr/bin/certbot -a \! -d /run/systemd/system && perl -e 'sleep int(rand(43200))' && certbot -q renew
# <2>
```

1. **--dry-run**: Prueba renovación sin usar límites
2. **Cronjob**: Dos veces al día, automático

## Hardening de Nginx

### 1. Ocultar Información

```nginx
# En server block o http block
server_tokens off;           # Ocultar versión
more_clear_headers Server;    # Ocultar "Server" header

# En http block
client_max_body_size 10M;     # Limitar upload
client_body_timeout 60s;      # Timeout de body
# <1>
```

1. **server_tokens off**: Solo muestra "nginx"

### 2. Security Headers

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf <<'EOF'
# Security Headers
add_header X-Frame-Options "SAMEORIGIN" always;
add_header X-Content-Type-Options "nosniff" always;
add_header X-XSS-Protection "1; mode=block" always;
add_header Referrer-Policy "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" always;
add_header Permissions-Policy "geolocation=(), microphone=(), camera=()" always;

# CSP (ajustar según aplicación)
# add_header Content-Security-Policy "default-src 'self';" always;
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Headers de seguridad**: Los 5 esenciales

### 3. Rate Limiting

```nginx
# Definir limit zone
http {
    limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr zone=api_limit:10M rate=10r/s;
    
    # En location
    location /api/ {
        limit_req zone=api_limit burst=20 nodelay;
    }
}
# <1>
```

1. **Rate limiting**: 10 requests por segundo, burst de 20

### 4. Proteger Archivos Sensibles

```nginx
# Bloquear archivos sensibles
location ~ /\.(htaccess|htpasswd|git|env|config|ini|xml|json|md|yml) {
    deny all;
}

# Bloquear directory listing
location / {
    try_files $uri $uri/ =404;
    autoindex off;
}
```

### Configuración Completa de Producción

```bash
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/production.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 80;
    listen [::]:80;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    
    # Redirect to HTTPS
    return 301 https://$server_name$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    listen [::]:443 ssl http2;
    server_name example.com www.example.com;
    
    # SSL
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;
    
    # Root y index
    root /var/www/example.com/public;
    index index.php index.html;
    
    # Security headers
    include /etc/nginx/snippets/security-headers.conf;
    
    # Logs
    access_log /var/www/example.com/logs/access.log;
    error_log /var/www/example.com/logs/error.log;
    
    # Directorio público
    location / {
        try_files $uri $uri/ /index.php?$query_string;
    }
    
    # PHP-FPM (si aplica)
    location ~ \.php$ {
        include snippets/fastcgi-php.conf;
        fastcgi_pass unix:/run/php/php8.1-fpm.sock;
        fastcgi_param SCRIPT_FILENAME $document_root$fastcgi_script_name;
        include fastcgi_params;
    }
    
    # Static files con cache
    location ~* \.(jpg|jpeg|png|gif|ico|css|js|woff|woff2|ttf|svg)$ {
        expires 30d;
        add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
    }
    
    # Denegar acceso a privados
    location /private/ {
        deny all;
    }
}
EOF
# <1>
```

1. **Configuración producción**: SSL, headers, PHP-FPM, cache

## Casos de Estudio

### Caso 1: Node.js API Detrás de Nginx

**Escenario**: API en Node.js puerto 3000, expuesta via Nginx con SSL.

```
Internet → Nginx (:443) → Node.js (:3000)
```

```bash
# Backend Node.js corriendo
$ ps aux | grep node
node server.js  # PID 5678, puerto 3000

# Config Nginx
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/sites-available/nodejs-api.conf <<'EOF'
server {
    listen 443 ssl http2;
    server_name api.example.com;
    
    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/privkey.pem;
    
    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
        
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
EOF

$ sudo ln -sf /etc/nginx/sites-available/nodejs-api.conf /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
$ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx

# Probar
$ curl -k https://api.example.com
{"status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"}
# <1>
```

1. **API funcionando**: Nginx proxyificando a Node.js

### Caso 2: Múltiples Dominios con Nginx

**Escenario**: 3 dominios servidos por el mismo Nginx.

```
site1.example.com → /var/www/site1
site2.example.com → /var/www/site2  
api.example.com   → proxy a :3000
```

```bash
# Site 1
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name site1.example.com;
    root /var/www/site1;
    index index.html;
}

# Site 2
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name site2.example.com;
    root /var/www/site2;
    index index.html;
}

# API
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name api.example.com;
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
}
# <1>
```

1. **Múltiples server blocks**: Cada uno con su dominio

### Caso 3: Nginx como Cache

**Escenario**: Cachear respuestas de API para reducir carga en backend.

```bash
# Habilitar proxy_cache
$ sudo tee /etc/nginx/conf.d/cache.conf <<'EOF'
proxy_cache_path /var/cache/nginx levels=1:2 keys_zone=api_cache:10m max_size=100m inactive=60m use_temp_path=off;

proxy_cache_key "$scheme$request_method$host$request_uri";
proxy_cache_valid 200 60m;
proxy_cache_valid 404 1m;
proxy_cache_use_stale error timeout updating http_500 http_502 http_503 http_504;
proxy_cache_lock on;

add_header X-Cache-Status $upstream_cache_status;
EOF

# Usar en location
location /api/ {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3000;
    proxy_cache api_cache;
    proxy_cache_valid 200 60m;
}
# <1>
```

1. **Cache de 60 min**: Respuestas 200 cacheadas

## Monitoreo y Logs

### Logs de Nginx

```bash
# Ver access log
$ tail -f /var/log/nginx/access.log

# Ver error log
$ tail -f /var/log/nginx/error.log

# Logs específicos por sitio
$ tail -f /var/www/example.com/logs/access.log

# Análisis rápido
$ awk '{print $9}' /var/log/nginx/access.log | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
# Status codes: 200 OK, 301 Redirect, 404 Not Found, 500 Error
```

### Monitoreo con systemd

```bash
# Estado
$ systemctl status nginx

# Recursos
$ systemctl show nginx | grep -E "Memory|Tasks|CPU"
```

### Health Check

```bash
# Endpoint de health
location /nginx-health {
    access_log off;
    return 200 "healthy\n";
    add_header Content-Type text/plain;
}
```

## Resumen

| Tema | Comando Clave | Propósito |
|------|---------------|-----------|
| Instalar | `apt install nginx` | Servidor web y proxy |
| Server Block | `/etc/nginx/sites-available/` | VirtualHosts |
| Validar | `nginx -t` | Test de sintaxis |
| Proxy | `proxy_pass` | Reverse proxy |
| SSL | `certbot --nginx` | TLS con Let's Encrypt |
| Hardening | `server_tokens off` | Ocultar información |
| Headers | `add_header X-Frame-Options` | Seguridad HTTP |
| Rate Limit | `limit_req_zone` | Prevenir DoS |

## Referencias

- [Nginx Documentation](https://nginx.org/en/docs/)
- [Nginx Admin Guide](https://docs.nginx.com/nginx/admin-guide/)
- [Mozilla SSL Configuration](https://ssl-config.mozilla.org/)
- [Certbot Documentation](https://certbot.eff.org/)
